paper_title: Ice Core Trace Metal Analysis Methods

methods.description: Rigorous acid-cleaning procedures for various plastic (PFA, FEP, PE, PC) and ceramic equipment used in ice core processing and analysis to minimize trace metal contamination. These procedures are based on previously published work or adapted from routine cleaning procedures.

steps.description: New perfluroalkoxy (PFA) or fluorinated ethylene propylene (FEP) Teflon filter rigs, vials, jars, bottles and micro centrifuge tubes were cleaned by sequential immersion in weak (B5%) Decon detergent (24 h), warm 50% (v/v) reagent-grade HNO 3 (24-48 h) and warm 50% (v/v) reagent-grade HCl (24-48 h).
steps.searchable_summary: Clean new PFA/FEP Teflon equipment by sequential immersion in detergent, HNO3, and HCl.

steps.description: Teflon equipment was re-cleaned by immersion in either warm 50% (v/v) HNO 3 or HCl (24 h).
steps.searchable_summary: Re-clean used PFA/FEP Teflon equipment by immersion in HNO3 or HCl.

steps.description: All Teflon equipment was thoroughly rinsed with ultrapure water following each step and then left to dry on a clean drying rack under laminar flow.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse Teflon equipment with ultrapure water and dry under laminar flow.

steps.description: For any Teflon equipment that was to be in contact with ice or samples at near-neutral pH, the plastic was filled with ultrapure water to condition the surface.
steps.searchable_summary: Condition Teflon equipment for near-neutral pH contact by filling with ultrapure water.

steps.description: Nalgene low-density PE and polycarbonate (PC) bottles were cleaned following previously published cleaning methods for preparation of equipment for seawater sampling 45 : bottles were sequentially immersed in weak (B5%) Decon detergent (48 h), 1 M reagent-grade HCl (48 h), 6 M reagent-grade HCl (48 h) and B1% (v/v) QD-HCl (43 months), with extensive rinsing with ultrapure water between steps.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean Nalgene PE/PC bottles by sequential immersion in detergent, 1M HCl, 6M HCl, and QD-HCl, with rinsing.

steps.description: Bottles were conditioned to neutral pH by filling with ultrapure water for 1 week before use.
steps.searchable_summary: Condition Nalgene bottles to neutral pH by filling with ultrapure water.

steps.description: Whatman Nucleopore PC membranes for filtration (0.2 mm) were cleaned by immersion in 6 M QD-HCl (48 h) and then rinsed extensively with ultrapure water.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean PC filtration membranes by immersion in QD-HCl and rinsing.

steps.description: Membranes were always handled with clean PFA tweezers to minimize contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: Handle filtration membranes using clean PFA tweezers.

steps.description: Membranes were stored in and flushed with ultrapure water before use to condition the plastic to near-neutral pH for contact with ice samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Store and flush PC membranes with ultrapure water before use to condition for near-neutral pH.

steps.description: Pipette tips were either acid-cleaned 45 with 50% (v/v) reagent-grade HCl (24 h) and rinsed with ultrapure water (seawater samples), or rinsed twice with 10% (v/v) QD-HNO 3 and twice with ultrapure water before use (non-seawater samples).
steps.searchable_summary: Clean pipette tips using acid (HCl or QD-HNO3) and rinse with ultrapure water, depending on sample type.

steps.description: Kyocera ceramic knives and polytetrafluroethylene (PTFE) Teflon boards were used for handling and decontamination of ice samples. Knives were cleaned by sequential immersion in 6 M reagent-grade HCl (24 h) and 1 M reagent-grade HCl (24 h), followed by extensive rinsing with ultrapure water.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean ceramic knives by sequential immersion in 6M HCl and 1M HCl, followed by rinsing.

steps.description: PTFE boards were cleaned by immersion in 6 M reagent-grade HCl and then rinsed extensively with ultrapure water.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean PTFE boards by immersion in 6M HCl and rinsing.

steps.description: Arm-length PE gloves for handling ice were cleaned by immersion in weak (B0.01% v/v) QD-HCl (24 h) and rinsed thoroughly with ultrapure water to ensure no transfer of acid to samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean arm-length PE gloves by immersion in weak QD-HCl and rinsing.

methods.method_name: Ice Core Sample Cutting and Decontamination
methods.description: Procedure for cutting ice core sections into subsamples and physically removing the outer contaminated layers using ceramic blades to prepare them for trace metal analysis.

steps.description: EDC samples from 21 to 26 kyr BP were chosen to cover the very high dust portion of the LGM... Berkner samples were chosen to cover MIS 2-3...
steps.searchable_summary: Select ice core samples based on age, depth, and scientific objectives.
inputs: Age scale data (EDC3, Berkner dD record)

steps.description: 'A cuts' (or B 1 4 of the core) from 15 separate 'bags' of ice (55 cm vertical section; B50 years) from the EDC ice core were supplied by EPICA... Ice was supplied as 1 4 pieces of Berkner core (diameter 50 mm)...
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain pre-cut sections (e.g., 1/4 core) of ice from archive.

steps.description: For EDC sampling, B3 cm pieces were typically cut from either the bottom or top of each bag, using a steel bandsaw within the cold room ( À 20°C) at the British Antarctic Survey.
steps.searchable_summary: Cut B3 cm vertical pieces from ice core sections using a bandsaw in a cold room.

steps.description: Two vertical cuts were then made to create three subsamples of identical age ( Supplementary Fig. 2b), which were then taken for decontamination and processing...
steps.searchable_summary: Make two vertical cuts on B3 cm pieces to create three subsamples of identical age.
outputs: Three ice core subsamples of identical age

steps.description: Precise age for each 3 cm sample was calculated from depth within each bag and the EDC3 age scale 47 .
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate precise age for each sample using depth and age scale.

steps.description: Each piece of ice was placed on a PTFE board and held with a clean PE glove, while the outside few mm were scraped off with a clean ceramic blade.
steps.searchable_summary: Place ice piece on a clean PTFE board, hold with a clean PE glove, and scrape off outer few mm using a clean ceramic blade.

steps.description: This was repeated three times using separate clean equipment.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat the scraping process three times using separate sets of clean equipment.

steps.description: To achieve the lowest contamination levels, all ice sampling equipment and ice were only handled with clean PE gloves, and all ice decontamination was carried out under Class 100 laminar flow air within the cold room ( À 20°C) at the British Antarctic Survey.
steps.searchable_summary: Handle all ice and equipment with clean PE gloves and perform decontamination under Class 100 laminar flow air in a cold room.

methods.description: A procedure for completely dissolving decontaminated ice samples, including any dust particles, using a multi-acid digestion process to prepare them for total elemental analysis by ICP-OES.

steps.description: Following decontamination, ice samples for total digestion were allowed to melt within PFA vials and acidified with 50 ml of QD-HNO 3 (conc.) per 5 ml.
steps.searchable_summary: Melt decontaminated ice samples in PFA vials and acidify with concentrated QD-HNO3.

steps.description: A 5-ml aliquot was evaporated to dryness, refluxed overnight at B80°C with 100 ml QD-HNO 3 (conc.) and 200 ml Teflon-distilled conc. HF, evaporated to dryness, redissolved in 100 ml of 6 mol l À 1 QD-HCl, refluxed at B80°C overnight and evaporated to dryness.
steps.searchable_summary: Take aliquot, evaporate, reflux with HNO3/HF, evaporate, redissolve in HCl, reflux, and evaporate to dryness.

steps.description: Finally, samples were redissolved in 250-500 ml of 0.1 mol l À 1 QD-HNO 3 for analysis by inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry (ICP-OES).
steps.searchable_summary: Redissolve digested residue in 0.1 mol/L QD-HNO3 for ICP-OES analysis.

methods.description: A procedure to process decontaminated ice samples for soluble elemental analysis by melting at natural pH (~5.3) and rapidly filtering to separate the liquid phase from dust, followed by acidification and preparation for ICP-OES.

steps.description: Following decontamination, ice samples for rapid filtration were allowed to melt at room temperature (B2 h) on a 0.2-mm Whatman polycarbonate membrane inside a 47-mm PFA Savillex filter rig, under vacuum.
steps.searchable_summary: Melt decontaminated ice samples on a PC membrane in a PFA filter rig under vacuum at room temperature.
outputs: Melting ice sample on filter rig

steps.description: Rigs were covered to prevent contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: Cover filter rigs to prevent contamination during melting/filtration.
inputs: PFA Savillex filter rig with sample

steps.description: Liquid that formed was quickly filtered and collected in a PFA reservoir containing an appropriate volume of conc. HNO 3 to acidify samples to pH 0.
steps.searchable_summary: Quickly filter meltwater and collect in a PFA reservoir containing concentrated HNO3 to acidify.
inputs: Melting ice sample on filter rig

steps.description: On completion of melting, dust was leached with three 25 ml aliquots of ultrapure water, similarly acidified.
steps.searchable_summary: Leach remaining dust on the filter with three aliquots of ultrapure water, acidifying each aliquot.

steps.description: Acidified samples were evaporated to dryness and redissolved in 300 ml of 0.1 mol l À 1 QD-HNO 3 for analysis by ICP-OES.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaporate acidified samples (meltwater + leachates) to dryness and redissolve in 0.1 mol/L QD-HNO3 for ICP-OES analysis.

steps.description: Fe concentration from the meltwater and subsequent ultrapure leaches were combined in final calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine elemental concentrations measured in the meltwater and leachate fractions for final calculation.

methods.description: Analytical procedure using Inductively Coupled Plasma Optical Emission Spectrometry (ICP-OES) to measure the concentrations of dissolved Al, Ca, Fe, and other cations in prepared ice core samples (from total digestion or rapid filtration).

steps.description: Samples from total digestion or rapid filtration were analysed for dissolved Al, Ca, Fe and other cations using a Varian Vista ICP-OES at the University of Cambridge.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze prepared samples for elemental concentrations using ICP-OES.
inputs: Sample solution for ICP-OES (from total digestion or rapid filtration)

steps.description: Each sample was analysed six times and a mean concentration was determined by reference to a quadratic calibration curve based on five standards.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform six replicate analyses per sample and calculate mean concentration using a quadratic calibration curve.

steps.description: Single-session accuracy on a 250-ng per gram internal multi-element reference standard for Al, Ca and Fe was 499.6% with precision of 0.41% (1 s.d.; n ¼ 10). Long-term reproducibility of the standard was 100±1% for all elements (n ¼ 60).
steps.searchable_summary: Assess analytical accuracy and precision using internal and long-term reference standards.

steps.description: Detection limits for Fe were 0.1-0.4 ng g À 1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Determine or note detection limits for analyzed elements.

steps.description: We assign 5% uncertainty on data to account for weighing, pipetting and %RSD of six ICP-OES analyses (typically o5%).
steps.searchable_summary: Assign overall uncertainty to the final concentration data.

methods.description: A method to extract dust from ice core material by sublimation, avoiding melting and potential alteration of dust speciation/solubility, designed to be representative of dry dust deposition.

steps.description: We designed a sublimation apparatus based on previous studies on gases 58 , adapted to cleanly extract large masses of dust 57 from ice without melting.
steps.searchable_summary: Assemble sublimation apparatus consisting of a desiccator, vacuum pump, and freeze trap.
inputs: Vacuum pump with oil cleaner and pressure gauge

steps.description: In the present study, ice samples were placed directly on membranes within filter rigs within a Savillex PFA Teflon Jar with top ports open (25-50 g; Supplementary Fig. 3a).
steps.searchable_summary: Place ice samples (25-50 g) on membranes within filter rigs inside a PFA Teflon jar with open ports.
outputs: Ice sample loaded into sublimation jar

steps.description: Sublimation allows the extraction of gases or dust from ice-core material while preventing melting, which could compromise the sample fidelity, and is achieved by lowering the pressure and temperature of ice below the triple point (0.01°C, 6.1173 mbar).
steps.searchable_summary: Operate the sublimation apparatus to lower pressure and temperature below the triple point, causing ice to sublimate.

steps.description: ...a À 100°C freeze trap for removal of water vapour...
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sublimated water vapor in the freeze trap.
